## Expected Behavior

## Actual Behavior

<!-- What actually happens or is stated instead? How does the current state deviate from what is intended? -->

## Severity

<!-- Select the severity level. Definitions are anchored to Algorapolis governance concepts. -->

- [ ] **Critical** — Constitutional constraint violation; a guardrail is bypassed, a human-reserved domain is encroached upon, or the Sovereign Logic Engine would produce unsafe outputs. The integrity of the entire governance architecture is at risk.
- [ ] **High** — Governance failure; a sandbox produces unreliable simulations, the Emotional Intelligence Architecture yields harmful assessments, or the National Digital Twin diverges dangerously from reality. Core functionality of a layer is compromised.
- [ ] **Medium** — Functionality degradation; a module does not perform at its specified fidelity, cross-layer coordination is weakened, or offline-resilience guarantees are partially broken. The system still operates but with reduced integrity.
- [ ] **Low** — Cosmetic; formatting, typographical, or presentational issues in documentation, notation inconsistencies, or minor clarity improvements that do not affect governance logic or constitutional compliance.

## Algorapolis-Specific Compliance Check

**Does this bug affect any of the Four Guardrails or Six Human-Reserved Domains?**

- [ ] Yes — this bug involves a Guardrail violation (please specify below)
- [ ] Yes — this bug involves a Human-Reserved Domain encroachment (please specify below)
- [ ] No — this bug is contained within non-constitutional layers

<!-- If yes, elaborate on which specific guardrail(s) or domain(s) are affected and how. -->

**Four Guardrails:**
1. No algorithm may override human democratic will
2. All systems must preserve offline functionality
3. No personal data may be used without explicit sovereign consent
4. Emotional data may inform but never dictate governance decisions

**Six Human-Reserved Domains:**
1. Moral judgment
2. Cultural identity
3. Spiritual belief
4. Artistic expression
5. Family structure
6. Personal purpose
